dublinCore.dc:title: 119 HR 6495 : Taxpayer Notification and Privacy Act
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

form.current-chamber: IN THE SENATE OF THE UNITED STATES

action.action-desc: Received; read twice and referred to the Committee on Finance

form.official-title: To amend the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 to provide for specific taxpayer notice when information is sought from third parties.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Taxpayer Notification and Privacy Act.

subsection.text: Paragraph (1) of section 7602(c) of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 is amended—

paragraph.text: by striking and at the end of subparagraph (A),
by inserting after subparagraph (A) the following new subparagraph:

subparagraph.text: in any case in which the information sought to be obtained from such other persons is related to determining tax liability, has not been previously requested from the taxpayer, and could reasonably be provided by the taxpayer, identifies each specific item of information intended to be sought from such persons, and

paragraph.text: by amending subparagraph (C), as redesignated by paragraph (2), to read as follows:

subparagraph.text: except as otherwise provided by the Secretary, provides the taxpayer with reasonable opportunity and a period of not less than 45 days (or more, if the taxpayer requests additional time and shows reasonable cause) to respond before contact is made with such other persons.

subsection.text: Section 7602(c)(3) of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 is amended—

paragraph.text: by redesignating subparagraphs (A), (B), and (C) as clauses (i), (ii), and (iii), respectively, and by moving such clauses 2 ems to the right,
by striking Exceptions.—This subsection and inserting “Exceptions.—
by adding at the end the following new subparagraph:

subparagraph.text: Subparagraph (B) of paragraph (1) shall not apply to information sought from a person other than the taxpayer if the Secretary determines that such information is necessary.

subsection.text: The amendments made by this section shall apply to notices provided under section 7602(c) of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 after the date that is 12 months after the date of the enactment of this Act.

attestation-group.attestation-date: Passed the House of Representatives April 27, 2026.
